texto: Continued observations in clinical psychological practice lead almost inevitably to the conclusion that deeper and more fundamental than sexuality, deeper than the craving for social power, deeper even than the desire for possessions, there is a still more generalized and universal craving in the human make-up. It is the craving for knowledge of the right direction - for orientation.
explicacao: Citação de William Sheldon sobre temas ligados a sabedoria. “Continued observations in clinical psychological practice lead almost inevitably to the conclusio…” articula sabedoria, desire, knowledge em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como knowledge e power.
